new_jobs.title: Deputy Program Director
new_jobs.description:
Who We Are

The Council of State Governments Justice Center helps leaders in every state design and implement solutions for complex justice system challenges and make communities safer and healthier. We deliver trusted data, practical strategies, and cross-systems collaboration to create second chances, reduce harm, and transform lives.

We're Focused On Four Strategic Priorities

Using data to drive action
Strengthening crisis response and addressing violent crime
Turning billions in new behavioral health resources into improved outcomes
Growing the number of states committed to delivering reentry success by 2030

We have approximately 50 employees in states across the country who have decades of experience working in and with criminal, juvenile, and Tribal justice agencies. We have provided housing, mental health, and addiction services; served as educators and in workforce development agencies; and supported victims and survivors of crime. We have served governors, state legislators, and members of Congress on both sides of the political spectrum. What binds us together is a shared commitment to our mission; to one another; to a common set of values; and to the national, bipartisan advisory board that guides our work.

Learn more about careers at the CSG Justice Center.

How You'll Fit In

The Deputy Program Director provides high-level strategic leadership across major policy initiatives and serves as a recognized expert in their field. Individuals in this role conceptualize, develop, and oversee complex, multi-year national or statewide projects; guide high-ranking policymakers and agency leaders; and ensure the delivery of impactful technical assistance, tools, and written products that shape policy and practice at scale. The Deputy Program Director defines strategic vision, establishes program goals, coordinates diverse internal and external contributors, and represents the organization in high-profile settings.

The Deputy Program Director build and lead teams, steward advisory groups and national partnerships, and lead fundraising and business development. They collaborate closely with senior organizational leadership to identify new opportunities, develop initiatives, and advance the mission. This role requires significant autonomy, strategic judgment, and deep subject matter expertise in one or more areas relevant to the policy team's work. At this time, the CSG Justice Center is seeking qualified candidates with significant expertise and experience in the following areas: law enforcement and crisis response (including community-led response, co-response, mobile crisis); courts (including problem-solving courts, diversion programs); policies and programs for young people at risk of- or involved with- the juvenile justice system; behavioral health treatment and financing; and housing.

Location:
NYC metro area preferred (hybrid); remote candidates will also be considered.

What You'll Do

Specific duties and responsibilities of this role include, but are not limited to, the following:

Strategic Leadership & Program Development

Design and execute strategic plans and work plans for major initiatives, aligning program goals with organizational priorities and partner needs.
Conceptualize and develop new policy initiatives, resources, and program models to address emerging or systemic challenges.
Establish program vision and communicate strategic goals and results to national audiences through presentations, publications, and convenings.

Advanced Field Expertise & High-Level Technical Assistance

Engage credibly with programs, local and state government agencies, and justice, health, and housing system stakeholders, including judges, prosecutors, defense attorneys, law enforcement, and agency leaders, to identify and prioritize areas of technical assistance that are needed and welcome; provide that assistance directly or connect grantees to the appropriate team member or consultant.
Lead or facilitate policy-focused meetings, forums, trainings, convenings, and events with senior government officials, advocates, and subject matter experts.
Translate identified field needs into new program initiatives, analyses, training curricula, or policy guidance tools that advance the policy team's mission and create value for grantees and partners.

External Engagement, Advisory Leadership & Partnerships

Manage advisory boards, high-level stakeholder groups, and networks of subject matter experts to ensure initiatives benefit from broad and diverse expertise.
Cultivate and sustain strategic relationships with policymakers, funders, agency leaders, and national partners.
Provide updates, insights, and emerging findings that strengthen external relationships and enhance project impact.

Written Products, Resources & Communications

Ensure high quality for written products, tools, and presentations that influence policy and practice at scale.
Ensure all materials meet the organization's standards for clarity, accuracy, accessibility, and strategic alignment.
Contribute to shaping public-facing messaging and resources that communicate policy priorities and progress.

Project Oversight, Quality Assurance & Accountability

Monitor timelines, deliverables, and reporting requirements to ensure funder obligations and strategic objectives are met.
Anticipate challenges, adjust plans accordingly, and ensure projects maintain momentum and high-quality standards.
Support coordination across internal teams (policy, research, data, communications, operations) to achieve integrated and effective project execution.

Staff Leadership & Team Development

Provide task direction, mentorship, and professional support to staff where applicable; may supervise direct reports depending on team structure.
Build and develop high-functioning teams by providing coaching, feedback, and opportunities for professional growth.
Promote an inclusive and collaborative culture grounded in interpersonal, institutional, and justice-focused leadership.

Fundraising, Business Development & Organizational Contribution

Lead and contribute to fundraising and business development efforts, including identifying and cultivating new funding opportunities, writing and contributing to grant proposals and concept papers, and stewarding relationships with government and philanthropic funders to sustain and grow the program portfolio.
Support organizational leadership in developing new projects or expanding existing initiatives.
Engage in internal conversations, committees, and strategic planning efforts that strengthen organizational systems and mission impact.

What You'll Bring

Deep subject matter expertise in one or more areas relevant to the policy team's work, which may include court-based diversion, problem-solving courts, behavioral health, crisis response, housing, health financing, or services for young people; demonstrated ability to apply that expertise in high-level consultation, program design, and field leadership
Demonstrated ability to conceptualize, propose, fund, staff, and launch new programs that strategically advance the organization's mission
Demonstrated experience leading a collection of projects or programs through strategy, oversight, and direction
Demonstrated experience writing proposals, and cultivating and stewarding relationships with government and philanthropic funders to sustain and grow a program portfolio
Demonstrated track record in business development, including identifying and pursuing new funding opportunities, cultivating relationships with government and philanthropic funders, and leading or contributing to successful grant proposals and concept papers
Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams, coordinate work across internal and external partners, and advance program goals in a project leadership capacity
Minimum 8 years of relevant work experience and/or equivalent educational achievements

How You Apply

If you're interested in helping us drive change across the country, you should upload the following elements with your application:

Cover letter
Resume (this should include a comprehensive list of related professional, internship, and volunteer experience with applicable dates)
Writing sample
At least three professional references

We will consider all paid and unpaid experience relevant to this position, including internships, fellowships, and volunteer activities.

Compensation will be based on experience and geography. The salary range for this position is $105,800.00 - $150,000.00
. Salary ranges are subject to change based on work location and market conditions.

Our organization offers flexibility in work arrangements, allowing for remote work or a hybrid model. Candidates residing in the NYC metropolitan area will be considered for hybrid model employment.

To learn more about careers at the CSG Justice Center, and about our comprehensive benefit offerings, including medical, dental, vision, retirement, paid time off, PSLF eligibility and more, please visit our website here .

Position Summary

Director of Advancement Services is a strategic and operational leader responsible for managing the revenue processing/gift administration team within the Operations Division of the Office of External Affairs (Development) and serves as a key partner in all areas of institutional advancement. This role is pivotal in ensuring the integrity of EA/Development Office policies, procedures, and CRM system, and leveraging these resources to drive strategic fundraising priorities and data-informed decision making. The Director will play a key role in long range and short-term planning in areas including database management and integrity, gift processing, demo/bio management, reporting and data analysis, and system and business process enhancement.

Job Responsibilities

Member of EA Ops leadership team; provides info/guidance to fundraising leadership on progress, industry trends, legislation, etc. Collaborates regularly with other Advancement directors. Serves as resource to various internal/external constituencies.
Oversees end-to-end cycle of revenue processing from gift acceptance through booking, transfer, receipting, reconciliation, reporting. Leads strategic and tactical planning for unit. Hires, trains, manages staff; develops/refines business processes.
Interacts regularly with donors, advisors, fiduciaries, Finance, Budget, faculty, mission deans, gift officers, and colleagues at NYP and CU-Ithaca regarding gift transactions, restrictions, delivery of assets to the institution.
Manages WCM compliance with IRS rules, regulatory reporting (DOE, charitable registrations, etc.) and industry best practices (CASE, FASB, etc.) for gift acceptance, processing, receipting, reporting. Develops/enforces relevant policies/procedures.
Ensures complex contributions such as non-federal grants, non-marketable assets are handled accurately and compliantly. Collaborates with OGC on gift agreements and complex philanthropic transactions, and with Trusts Office on estate administration.
Provides strategic leadership in partnering with Finance Dept to create stronger synergies and more effective processes. Collaborates with ITS and CRM/technology leadership on software integrations, implementations, and serves as SME on CEMI project teams.
Communicates regularly with Finance, dept business officers, and other administrators, providing counsel on matters including gift handling, fund creation and management, revenue classification, gift acceptance, accounting policies and procedures, etc.
Designs processes to optimize workflows; devises technological solutions such as custom reports and dashboards to monitor performance and quality control. Partners with tech staff to produce BI reports and visualizations for internal/external audiences.
Partners closely with Prospect Management and Donor Relations staff in use, refinement, and enhancement of moves management protocols and analyses, demo/bio standards, donor stewardship initiatives, and end-user CRM training.
Maintains professional affiliations and participates in professional associations (AASP, AFP, AHP, etc.) to expand knowledge and remain current on issues affecting non-profit gift administration for higher ed/healthcare.

Education

Bachelor's degree required
Advance degree preferred

Experience

At least 10 years of experience working in advancement services/development operations in higher education, academic medicine, or similarly complex or large organization (including at least 5 years of experience managing staff).
Comfort with complexity, ambiguity, and working in a fast-paced environment.

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities

Deep understanding and knowledge of best practices in fundraising and advancement services.
Expertise in the management of information systems/CRMs, relational databases, and reporting/analysis/project management. Strong knowledge of fundraising CRMs (Blackbaud, Salesforce, etc.)
Exceptional skills in written and oral communication, critical thinking, data analysis, problem solving, detail orientation, and quality assurance.
Ability to deal effectively with a broad range of customers including donors, VIPs, senior administrators, legal counsel, mission deans, IT and Finance professionals, and frontline fundraisers.
Knowledge of relevant regulations (IRS, NYPMIFA, GDPR, etc.) and reporting standards (CASE, FASB, GAAP, etc.) applicable to advancement services.

Licenses and Certifications

Availability to work outside of normal business hours, as needed (especially during calendar and fiscal year-end).

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
About Campus

Campus is on a mission to accelerate the talent of tomorrow and reimagine what college can (and should) be. The problem is clear: college costs have skyrocketed, but outcomes haven’t. Traditional college hasn’t kept up with the aspirations of today’s students—and they can’t afford to wait.

At Campus, we’ve built a two-year, accredited college where students learn from top professors at universities like Princeton, Stanford, and Howard, in live, online classes. Our students graduate with in-demand business and AI skills—ready to launch their careers or transfer to top four-year universities. Our unique approach is grounded in a pioneering technology platform and a heavily-researched student success model that offers students dedicated advisors and on-demand tutoring and coaching. We were also named one of Fast Company's Most Innovative Companies of 2024.

We’re backed by an exceptional investor team that believes deeply in our vision for the future of education, including General Catalyst, Founders Fund, Bloomberg Beta, 8VC, Rethink Education, Sam Altman, Jason Citron, Shaquille O’Neal, and others.

The world has changed. Higher ed needs to catch up, and we’re leading the way - come join us!

About The Role

We are looking for a Director of Operations
to lead and continuously improve one of Campus's most critical operational functions. In the near term, your primary focus will be Financial Aid and the operational processes that support the student financial experience. Over time, this role may expand into additional student-facing operational areas as Campus grows.

Reporting to the COO, you'll work closely with Financial Aid leaders and cross-functional partners to optimize day-to-day operations, solve bottlenecks, and design scalable processes that improve both the student and staff experience.

This is not a "keep the lights on" role. We're looking for an operator who knows how to improve and scale an existing function. You'll inherit a complex operation and be responsible for identifying bottlenecks, partnering closely with Product and Engineering, developing managers, and continuously improving systems and processes as Campus grows.

You're someone who enjoys taking an operation that's already working and making it significantly better. You use data, operational judgment, and strong cross-functional partnerships to improve how work gets done at scale—and you're energized by continuously refining and evolving an operation rather than simply maintaining it.

You’re Excited About This Opportunity Because You Will…

Partner with Financial Aid leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to improve and scale critical student-facing operations.
Serve as a strategic advisor to the COO on operational risks, opportunities, and resource planning.
Support critical processes such as aid applications, disbursements, billing, refunds, and payment plans — ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance.
Improve existing workflows, identify operational bottlenecks, and implement scalable processes that increase efficiency, quality, and team effectiveness.
Create and maintain SOPs that keep cross-functional teams (Admissions, Registrar, Student Success, Financial Aid, Product, etc.) aligned and consistent in how student-facing processes are executed.
Partner closely with Product and Engineering to design, implement, and drive adoption of technology-enabled operational improvements.
Establish performance metrics and dashboards to monitor progress, identify issues early, and continuously improve service.
Flex into new operational areas over time as Campus continues to scale.

new_jobs.description:
About our Organization:

Dow Jones is a global provider of news and business information, delivering content to consumers and organizations around the world across multiple formats, including print, digital, mobile and live events. Dow Jones has produced unrivaled quality content for more than 130 years and today has one of the world’s largest newsgathering operations globally.

It is home to leading publications and products including the flagship Wall Street Journal, America’s largest newspaper by paid circulation; Barron’s, MarketWatch, Mansion Global, Financial News, Dow Jones Risk & Compliance and Dow Jones Newswires. Dow Jones is a division of News Corp (Nasdaq: NWS, NWSA; ASX: NWS, NWSLV).

About the Role:

We're on a mission at Dow Jones to provide the world's most trusted journalism, data and analysis to help people make decisions. As part of the company's global communications team, you'll play a key role in leading and supporting our newsrooms, working collaboratively across both our team and executive leadership to support our journalism and tell our company story. You will help to lead and contribute to a team of talented communications professionals with a focus on strategy development, execution, media relations, crisis communications, reputation management and executive profile building. This is a multi-faceted role and one designed to bring critical skills, leadership abilities and a bias toward action to our team as we work to tell great stories about the company and its businesses. A key component to this role is supporting our consumer publications–The Wall Street Journal, Barron's, MarketWatch, Investor's Business Daily–developing communications strategies that support newsroom leaderships' vision, securing high-impact media coverage around key moments and initiatives, protecting our reputation, promoting our journalism and aligning the newsroom efforts to the broader mission of Dow Jones. You will partner with our editors in chief, managing editors, heads of strategy, digital, social and audio/video, among others, to tell compelling multimedia stories, both externally and internally among our newsrooms and across Dow Jones.

You Will:

Report to the SVP, Deputy Chief Communications Officer and be based in our New York City office.
Support and protect our news brands, driving proactive storytelling around some of the world's leading business publications and effectively positioning our journalists, editors and leaders as experts across the broader media landscape.
Drive external and internal communications for newsroom leaders, including getting stories placed, promoting our journalism, managing and building executive profiles, guiding change and protecting our brand reputation.
Lead and execute the global communications plan for our newsrooms, directly managing a NY-based team and working closely with communications peers in Asia and Europe to promote our journalism and protect our brand around the world.
Build and maintain strong relationships with key journalists, producers, event organizers and industry peers to create new opportunities to promote Dow Jones and our brands.
Provide effective media training for journalists to ensure effective and consistent messaging in external appearances.
Develop and execute a comprehensive executive speaking strategy, including identifying high-value external forums and events, and providing on-site communications support and briefing materials for executive participation.
Protect our company and brands' reputation by identifying risks and quickly preparing and issuing company responses.
Manage internal communications for our newsrooms, ensuring teams get important updates, and working closely with our corporate internal communications team on storytelling across our company-wide channels and platforms.
Play a key role in contributing to a strong team and company culture, taking to heart our values of collaboration, impact, trust and excellence.

You Have:

12+ years of communications experience in the news/media industry
Experience working with executive leaders on internal, external and crisis communications, preferably in a matrixed organization
A strategic, proactive mindset and a strong news judgment
Deep industry relationships and proven experience working with reporters, editors, producers and influencers
Strong written communication skills and the ability to craft compelling messages that cut through
Experience developing and executing impactful communications campaigns aligned with strategic priorities
A professional, collaborative and team-oriented approach to your work
Excellent project management skills and a solution-oriented mindset
Strong people management skills and an appetite to mentor and grow talent

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
Executive Director

Fund for Modern Courts

Location: Hybrid within New York State

The Fund for Modern Courts seeks its next Executive Director to lead one of New York’s most 
respected organizations dedicated to improving the administration of justice.
For more than 70 years, Modern Courts has been an independent, nonpartisan voice for a fair, 
accessible, and independent justice system. Through research, public education, advocacy, and civic 
engagement, Modern Courts works with judges, court leaders, legislators, bar associations, legal 
services providers, law firms, foundations, civic organizations, and community advocates to advance 
practical court reform.
The Position

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive of Modern Courts, working in partnership with an 
engaged Board of Directors. The Executive Director is also supported by a full-time Program Director 
who executes event logistics and back-office operations, allowing the Executive Director to focus on 
advocacy, fundraising, external relations, communications, and long-term strategy.
Key Responsibilities

The Executive Director will:

● Provide strategic leadership in partnership with the Board.
● Lead fundraising, including major gifts, grants, sponsorships, and signature events.
● Build relationships with judges, court leaders, legislators, law firms, bar associations, 
foundations, civic organizations, and other partners.
● Advance Modern Courts’ policy and advocacy agenda through research, coalition building, 
public education, and legislative engagement.
● Serve as the organization’s principal spokesperson.
● Oversee finances, operations, staff, and governance.
Qualifications

Successful candidates will bring many, though not necessarily all, of the following:
● Senior leadership experience in a nonprofit, legal, government, public policy, advocacy, or 
related organization.
● Fundraising, board engagement, and organizational leadership experience.
● Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
● Strong judgment, integrity, initiative, and an entrepreneurial leadership style.
● Ability to build relationships across diverse constituencies.
● Demonstrated interest in public service, access to justice, or improving government 
institutions. Familiarity with New York's courts, government, or public policy is preferred.
Bachelor's degree required. An advanced degree in law, public policy, public administration, nonprofit 
management, business, or a related field is welcomed but not required.
Compensation

Salary: $145,000–$165,000, commensurate with experience, with eligibility for a performance-based 
annual bonus as determined by the Board. Modern Courts offers generous paid vacation and holidays, 
employer-sponsored health insurance, access to a voluntary 401(k) plan, and a flexible hybrid work 
environment.
The Board is open to discussing full-time or part-time arrangements with highly qualified candidates.
To Apply

Please submit a resume and cover letter at link provided above
Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.
Equal Opportunity Employer

The Fund for Modern Courts is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applicants of all 
backgrounds. Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States

new_jobs.title: Executive Director - Fire Family Foundation
new_jobs.description:
This is a remote position. Candidates must live or work on Pacific Time zone hours. Western US candidates highly preferred.

Firefighters First Federal Credit Union has proudly been serving the firefighter community since 1935. We have built trusting relationships within the fire family as the nation’s largest exclusive firefighter credit union. Our commitment to providing our members with exceptional financial products and services, along with our legendary service, has allowed us to grow our membership nationally. Join a team that values its employees, invests in professional growth, and makes an impact in the firefighter community.

Currently, we have an immediate opening for an Executive Director
to lead our Fire Family Foundation! The Executive Director (ED) will serve as the head of the foundation dedicated to supporting firefighter families by addressing the emotional, psychological, and overall well-being challenges they face due to the inherent stress of the firefighting profession. The ED will provide visionary leadership, strategic direction, and operational oversight to ensure the foundation fulfills its mission. The ideal candidate has a deep understanding of the psychological challenges in firefighter families with a passion for promoting mental health and wellness. Additionally, the ED will focus on developing relationships with stakeholders and expanding the foundation’s funding and community reach through advocacy and fundraising.

Primary Responsibilities:

1. Leadership and Strategic Planning:
Develop and implement strategic initiatives to support firefighter families with a focus on mental health, family dynamics, and emotional resilience.
Lead with empathy and insight into the specific challenges faced by firefighter families, ensuring that the foundation’s programs are responsive to their unique needs.
Collaborate with the Board of Directors to establish long-term goals and ensure alignment with the foundation’s mission.

2. Program Development and Oversight:
Oversee the development and delivery of programs and events aimed at improving the well-being and mental health of firefighter families.
Stay informed about emerging trends in psychological health, trauma, and stress management, especially those affecting first responder families.
Partner with mental health professionals, social workers, and community organizations to offer effective services and support to firefighter families.

3. Fundraising and Resource Development:
Develop and implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy, including grant writing, individual donor cultivation, corporate partnerships, and events.
Actively seek out and secure new funding opportunities to ensure the financial sustainability of the foundation.
Build and nurture relationships with existing donors, sponsors, and potential philanthropic partners.

4. Community and Stakeholder Engagement:
Serve as the primary ambassador for the foundation, advocating for the mental health and well-being needs of firefighter families at public events, media outlets, and industry forums.
Cultivate relationships with fire departments, firefighter unions, community leaders, mental health professionals, and policymakers to advance the foundation’s mission.
Engage firefighter families directly to better understand their needs and ensure the foundation’s services remain relevant and impactful.

5. Financial and Operational Management:
Manage the foundation’s budget and resources to ensure efficient and effective program delivery.
Oversee all financial operations, including budget preparation, financial reporting, and compliance with nonprofit regulations.
Develop and implement policies, procedures, and operational frameworks that align with best practices for nonprofit management.

6. Board and Governance Relations:
Serve as the key liaison between the Board of Directors and Council Members and the foundation’s operations.
Collaborate with the board to ensure governance practices are in place, providing regular updates on the foundation’s progress, financial status, and strategic initiatives.
Assist in the recruitment and orientation of new board of directors and council members as needed.

Leadership Responsibilities:

Regularly meeting with each team member to coach and mentor to ensure alignment around the organization’s mission, vision, and values.
Providing team members with training, tools, and resources to successfully perform their jobs.
Holding teams accountable for meeting all service level agreements, ensuring the team’s work is accurate/efficient, and providing coaching, feedback, or training as needed.
Providing and ensuring development opportunities are available for team members as part of their overall career path.
Leading change by identifying opportunities that consistently raise the bar and improve service levels.
Assisting with executing the business plan of the foundation.

Basic Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Social Work, Psychology, Nonprofit Management, or related field
Minimum of 5 years of leadership experience in a nonprofit, mental health, or first responder support organization.
Deep understanding of the psychological and emotional challenges faced by firefighter families, such as trauma, PTSD, stress, and family dynamics.
Proven experience in fundraising, with a successful track record in securing grants, sponsorships, and individual contributions.
Strong leadership and team management skills, with the ability to inspire and motivate staff, volunteers, and partners.
Excellent communication and public speaking skills, with the ability to represent the foundation in public forums and media outlets.
Experience working with a Board of Directors and building governance structures for nonprofit organizations.
Compassionate, empathetic, and mission-driven with a passion for helping families in need.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
﻿ ProFound Executive Search is serving as an external search partner to The Arc of Chester County in its search for its Chief Executive Officer (CEO). This position reports to the Board of Directors, Board President at The Arc of Chester County.

About The Arc of Chester County

The Arc of Chester County is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that provides advocacy, education, and services in Chester County, Pennsylvania. Its mission is to advocate, educate and provide services that empower individuals with disabilities and their families to enhance the quality of their lives.

Position Summary

The CEO is responsible for the overall management and operations of the organization. The CEO will strategically work to enhance The Arc of Chester County’s ability to realize its mission and expand its reach as a provider of advocacy, education, and human services. The CEO will be responsible for the direct supervision of the Chief Officers of the organization. The CEO will oversee the development and implementation of the agency personnel policies to ensure they reflect best practices and comply with all laws and regulations. The CEO will partner with key internal and external stakeholders to maintain and improve the organization’s image and financial position. The CEO is responsible for providing support to the Board of Directors to assist the board as necessary in fulfilling their fiduciary and governing responsibilities. Other duties assigned by the Board.

As the organization enters its next chapter, it is building upon a remarkable 75-year legacy of programs, services, and advocacy with and for individuals with disabilities and their families. The disability sector is navigating an increasingly complex legislative and regulatory environment at the federal, state, and local levels. The Arc is well positioned to build on its legacy and continue as a trusted provider, advocate, and community partner.

Position Responsibilities

Essential Duties:

Embrace and exemplify The Arc of Chester County’s mission, vision, and core values.
Participate, collaborate and/or implement The Arc of Chester County’s 5-year strategic plan.
Complete all training and qualifications requirements.

Operations:

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Officers of the agency in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Collaborate with staff and leadership on agency operations plans and goals.
3. Seek and respond to recommendations for efficient and effective practices.
4. Ensure that the Chief Officers effectively manage and implement departmental and agency plans.
5. Guide others to develop operations-related policies, practices, standards, and security measures to ensure effective, appropriate, and consistent execution.
6. Present periodic performance reports and metrics to staff, the Board, and other relevant external and internal stakeholders.
7. Organize and attend Board meetings as required.
8. Assist the Board with volunteer recruitment and development; ensure staff support for Board and committees.
9. Research community partnerships that will promote the mission of the organization and maximize the image and presence of The Arc in the community.
10. Actively participate in community partnerships.

Programs and Services:

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Officer assigned to programs & services in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Oversee the implementation of strategic goals and outcomes for the programs and services.
3. Provide guidance and participate in program and service reviews, strategic planning and feasibility studies as needed.
4. Keep informed of changes and developments in the social services field, nonprofit requirements, funder requirements and best practices.
5. Monitor financial performance of programs and services.
6. Research business opportunities and share information with leadership for action.
7. Establish, cultivate, and maintain positive relationships with key decision makers and external leaders to advance the agency mission, goals, and strategic plans as they relate to programs and services.
8. Represent the organization by participating on relevant boards, committees and providing national, state, and local presentations, attend external events/meetings, and the like.

Fiscal Oversight:

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Oversee the agency fiscal contractor(s) in collaboration with the CFO to ensure the critical tasks of the contractors are fulfilled.
3. Ensure coordination and cooperation occurs between Chief Officer, management team, Finance and investment committees, and the board.
4. Ensure all fiscal requirements for a non-profit are met.
5. Assist with presenting the agency budgets to the board for approval and assist with the presentation of investments and finance statements to the Board.
6. Maintain and enhance the financial position for the agency through oversight, advocacy and ensuring implementation of strategic plans and goals.
7. Ensure contracted staff, Chief Officers and management collaborate to ensure maximum financial position for the agency and maximum wages and benefits for the staff is achieved.
8. Oversee the negotiations for services contracts and service rates and ensure that enhancing staff salaries are always a part of those negotiations.
9. Oversee all vendor proposal processes to ensure bids are obtained and process to select vendors is implemented.
10. Initiate and facilitate rates appeals, when applicable and appropriate.
11. Ensure the fiscal staff and HR staff analyze all staff salaries to market wages and plan and adjust salaries as the budget and financial position of the organization allows.
12. Continue ongoing efforts to increase administrative structure to achieve optimal table of organization.
13. Guide and assist with the development and implementation of financial and investment policies and procedures.

Development (fundraising, communications, and marketing):

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Development Officer (CDO) in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Guide and assist in the development and implementation of the organization’s comprehensive Development Plan.
3. Oversee the CDO so that the Development division has updated, compliant and best practice policies and procedures and ensure they are implemented consistently.
4. Guide and assist in the development of agency fundraising, communications, and marketing plans. 5. Support efforts to cultivate community partners, donors, major and planned gifts.
6. Guide and assist the CDO to cultivate new corporate partners and supporters of The Arc.

Quality and Compliance:

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Quality and Compliance Officer (CQ&CO) in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Monitor program quality and compliance to ensure the level of quality and compliance reflect the organization’s mission, vision, and core values.
3. Oversee the agency CQ&CO annual quality and compliance plans and activities.
4. Guide and assist the CQ&CO in the timely response of all required monitoring, licensing and quality and compliance activities.
5. Participate in Quality Enhancement Committee meetings as needed.
6. Guide and assist in the development and implementation of the organization’s Quality Management plan and other quality and risk management plans and activities.
7. Guide, assist and participate in the agency safety committee as needed to monitor the implementation of all safety goals and strategies.
8. Guide and oversee the quality and security of organization data systems.
9. Guide and participate in discussions and negotiations with the organization’s insurance broker.

Administration / Human Resources:

1. Recruit, supervise and evaluate the Chief Administration Officer (CAO) in accordance with the agency table of organization.
2. Monitor the development and implementation of quality human resource policies and procedures.
3. Oversee the CAO to ensure Human Resource records are maintained and kept confidential.
4. Guide and assist with the review and negotiations of employee benefits.
5. Guide and assist with the review of market wages and direct the execution to adjust staff wages to market wages.
6. Guide and assist with broker discussions for HR benefits.
7. Oversee, direct, and participate in legal matters related to HR issues.
8. Guide and assist with the development and implementation of policies and procedures that enhance onboarding, training plans, recruitment, and retention efforts.
9. Assist with the development of succession planning and efforts to cultivate future leaders of The Arc.
10. Monitor the administrative activities for the agency under the management of the CAO.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Nonprofit Management, Business, Human Services, Social Work, Disability Services, Special Education or related field
Minimum of 10 years of progressive leadership experience
Experience partnering with an executive team and Board of Directors
Experience leading, managing, and developing staff and teams
Demonstrated commitment to advocacy with and for the disability community

Preferred Qualifications:

Master’s degree
Nonprofit, human services, disability services, and/or special education experience
Experience managing a $25M+ budget
Experience developing programs
Experience fundraising
Experience developing strategic plans
Experience navigating legislative and regulatory environments
Experience building relationships with government agencies, external partners, funders, donors, vendors, etc.
Experience representing an organization as an ambassador

Salary Range:

$225,000 - $275,000

new_jobs.title: Chief Program Officer (CPO)
new_jobs.description:
MISSION, VISION & VALUES of WCA

All employees are expected to uphold and embody the mission, vision, and values of the Women’s and
Children’s Alliance (WCA) in all interactions with clients, colleagues, community partners, and donors.
MISSION: Safety, healing, and freedom from domestic abuse and sexual assault
VISION: To foster a community where individuals thrive in safe, healthy relationships
VALUES:
• Empowerment – fostering potential
• Respect – recognizing and honoring the dignity of all
• Integrity – consistent in word and deed

POSITION SUMMARY

The Chief Program Officer (CPO) is a key member of the Executive Team responsible for leading WCA’s
programmatic vision, service delivery strategy, and organizational impact. This role advances a cohesive,
high-quality system of care across residential and community-based programs while strengthening the
organization’s ability to deliver measurable, survivor-centered outcomes.
Building on WCA’s strong service foundation, the CPO leads an integrated program strategy that aligns clinical services, shelter operations, case management, court advocacy, and childcare into a seamless continuum of care. Central to this role is ensuring an exceptional client experience—one that is safe, trauma-informed, responsive, and grounded in dignity—while continuously measuring and improving client satisfaction and outcomes.

The CPO ensures programs are data-driven, culturally responsive, and aligned with evolving community
needs. This role brings a strategic and hands-on leadership approach, with the ability to move between

new_jobs.description:
At Habitat for Humanity Philadelphia (“HFHP”), we are builders who work alongside, not for others. We are driven by accountability and defined by our stick-to-itiveness.

Reporting directly to the CEO and serving as a member of the Leadership Team, the Chief Development Officer
(CDO) will partner closely with the CEO, Board of Directors, and Leadership Team to drive a comprehensive advancement strategy that strengthens philanthropic revenue, deepens donor engagement, elevates organizational visibility, and supports Habitat Philadelphia’s long-term strategic priorities.

The CDO is both strategic and hands-on leader who can architect sophisticated fundraising systems and campaigns while also building authentic relationships with donors, corporate partners, volunteers, and community stakeholders. This role requires a passionate, emotionally intelligent leader skilled in people management, strategic planning, and risk mitigation.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership

Develop and execute a comprehensive fundraising and advancement strategy aligned with Habitat Philadelphia’s strategic goals.
Implement diversified fundraising strategies to attract, retain, and motivate donors and volunteers.
Ensure fundraising alignment with organizational values and strategic plans.
Build fundraising infrastructure, team, systems, accountability measures, and performance metrics that support long-term sustainability and growth.
Foster a culture of philanthropy across the organization.
Monitor trends in philanthropy and recommend policy positions.
Represent Habitat Philadelphia externally with donors, civic leaders, corporate partners, volunteers, and community stakeholders.

Fund Development Oversight

Lead all philanthropic revenue strategies with accountability for annual fundraising goals and long-term revenue growth including annual fund, events, individual, foundation, corporate, and planned giving, volunteer engagement, marketing, and communications.
Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward major and transformational donors.
Manage donor portfolio.
Ensure compliance with regulations, ethical standards, and internal policies.

Represent Habitat to donors, prospects, and external stakeholders.

Support leadership development within the advancement function.
Establish clear accountabilities and conduct regular performance evaluations.

Partner with Board of Directors to achieve 100% participation with annual board giving.
Leverage expertise and engagement of Resource Development Committee.

Advancement Operations

Manage advancement budget, cash flow, projections; work in tandem with CFO.

Ensure strong development operations, data integrity, gift processing, stewardship systems, and performance tracking.
Utilize data and metrics to drive strategic fundraising decisions and organizational learning.

Campaign & Program Management

Design and implement cost-effective fundraising programs with strong ROI.
Lead major gift fundraising and multi-year campaigns, including strategy development, donor cultivation, and stewardship.

Ensure data integrity, fiscal responsibility, and adherence to ethical standards throughout campaigns.

new_jobs.description:
About Fort Worth Academy
At Fort Worth Academy, we believe philanthropy is about far more than fundraising—it is about inviting people to invest in possibility. We are an independent K–8 school that empowers young people to think critically, lead with empathy, and collaborate effectively to become the innovators and problem solvers of tomorrow. Through our signature Entrepreneurship, Leadership, and Innovation (ELI) initiative, Gold Standard Project-Based Learning, and highly personalized approach, students learn to know themselves, think deeply, create boldly, influence positively, and lead courageously. As we continue to build on a strong foundation—including a successful debt-free capital campaign, a growing endowment, and an ambitious vision for the future—we are seeking an exceptional leader to help tell our story and inspire others to invest in it.

The Opportunity
We're looking for someone who loves building relationships, telling meaningful stories, and connecting generous people with a mission that changes lives. The Director of Philanthropy & Community Engagement serves as the school's chief advancement leader, reporting directly to the Head of School and serving on the Leadership Team. This individual will lead the school's comprehensive advancement program while cultivating authentic relationships that strengthen our community and advance the mission of Fort Worth Academy.

Skills and Responsibilities
You are first and foremost a relationship builder. You possess exceptional emotional intelligence, communicate with authenticity, think strategically, and enjoy bringing people together around a meaningful mission. You are entrepreneurial, organized, collaborative, and inspired by the opportunity to help shape the future of an innovative school.

· Develop and implement a comprehensive advancement strategy aligned with the school's strategic plan.
· Lead the Annual Fund, major gifts, endowment growth, and future campaign initiatives.
· Cultivate and steward relationships with parents, grandparents, alumni, foundations, corporations, and
community partners.
· Partner closely with the Head of School and Board of Trustees to grow a culture of philanthropy.
· Collaborate with admissions and marketing to tell the Fort Worth Academy story through compelling
communications and donor engagement.
· Plan and oversee donor cultivation, stewardship, alumni, and community engagement events.
· Identify new opportunities for partnerships, sponsorships, and philanthropic investment.

Preferred Qualifications
· Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.
· Five or more years of advancement, philanthropy, nonprofit leadership, or related experience.
· Demonstrated success in donor cultivation, stewardship, and fundraising strategy.
· Outstanding writing, communication, presentation, and organizational skills.
· Experience working with volunteer boards and committees is preferred.

Why Fort Worth Academy?
Relationships come first at Fort Worth Academy. Students are known. Teachers are empowered. Innovation is encouraged. Leadership is practiced. Curiosity is celebrated.
This is an opportunity to become one of the school's primary storytellers and help build a culture of generosity that will shape generations of Trailblazers.

How to Apply
Please submit to employment@fwacademy.org your résumé along with a one-page letter describing a relationship you have built that inspired someone to invest in a meaningful mission or strengthened an organization or community. We're interested not only in what you've accomplished, but in how you build trust, cultivate generosity, and bring people together around a shared purpose.

new_jobs.title: Director of Grants & Proposals
new_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

Job Summary:
The Director of Strategic Grants & Proposals is a leadership role responsible for leading YFA’s grant development and proposal strategy. This position identifies, evaluates, and pursues public and private funding opportunities aligned with YFA’s mission, strategic priorities, organizational capacity, and growth goals.

As the organization’s lead grant writer and proposal development professional, the Director manages grant applications, government funding proposals, and competitive RFP responses from planning through submission. The Director works closely with operations, finance, quality improvement, and executive leadership teams to develop compelling narratives, budgets, and supporting materials that communicate organizational impact and funding needs.

In addition to the duties and requirements listed below, YFA expects employees to be committed to incorporating principles of trauma-informed care in all areas of service delivery. Commitment to these principles is demonstrated through active participation in agency-required trainings, use of trauma-informed practices, and understanding that trauma-informed environments require cultural sensitivity and an emphasis on a safe and respectful environment for all.

Essential Job Functions:

Conduct ongoing scans of local, state, federal, foundation, and corporate funding opportunities to identify potential revenue sources.

Evaluate funding opportunities for alignment with YFA’s mission, strategic plan, organizational capacity, and growth goals.

Lead go/no-go decision-making processes and coordinate proposal development activities across departments and programs.

Serve as the organization’s primary lead for drafting, editing, and submitting grant applications, funding proposals, and competitive RFP responses.

Manage all aspects of proposal development, including narrative creation, budget development, document collection, compliance review, quality assurance, packaging, and submission.

Assemble proposal development teams, assign responsibilities, and ensure accountability for deliverables.

Manage cross-functional grant and proposal teams to ensure timely completion of high-quality submissions.

Identify and track key proposal requirements, deadlines, compliance needs, and supporting documentation.

Develop and review grant and contract budgets in partnership with finance and program leadership.

Coordinate collection of organizational data, program outcomes, and performance metrics required for competitive submissions.

Conduct technical reviews of proposal narratives, attachments, and supporting documents to ensure quality, consistency, and compliance.

Facilitates the transition of approved grant and proposal requirements to the Director of Projects and Initiatives for implementation and oversight.

Provide training and technical assistance to staff on grant development, grants management, and funding best practices.

Develop and maintain proposal development tools, templates, and best practices to improve efficiency and quality.

Support relationship-building with funders, community partners, and collaborators to strengthen funding opportunities.

Promote a culture of high performance, continuous improvement, learning, and commitment to quality.

The above duties and responsibilities describe the general nature and level of work performed by people assigned to the job. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all duties and responsibilities that an incumbent may be expected or asked to perform.

REQUIREMENTS

Education & Experience
: 

Bachelor’s degree required; master’s degree preferred. At least five (5) years of progressive experience in grant writing, proposal development, fundraising, grants management, contract administration, or nonprofit development is required. Professional certifications, such as Grant Professional Certified (GPC), Certified Grants Management Specialist (CGMS), or Project Management Professional (PMP), are preferred but not required.

Preferred knowledge and understanding of trauma-informed principles and practices, including the importance of creating a safe environment, fostering trust, promoting collaboration, and empowering individuals.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities (KSAs):

Proven ability to lead strategic and operational grant development, proposal management, and administrative initiatives.

Ability to analyze funding trends, assess opportunities, and recommend pursuits aligned with organizational priorities.

Strong relationship-building skills with funders, community partners, collaborators, and internal stakeholders.

Creative and strategic thinker with the ability to develop and execute effective growth and funding strategies.

Ability to work independently, exercise sound judgment, and apply critical thinking in complex situations.

Excellent judgment and problem-solving skills, including negotiation and conflict resolution.

Effective communicator with the ability to work collaboratively with diverse staff, clients, partners, and the public.

Exceptional written, oral, interpersonal, and presentation skills, with the ability to engage executive leadership, the YFA Board of Directors, and staff.

Ability to facilitate meetings and group discussions while effectively managing varied group dynamics.

Ability to function effectively as both a leader and a contributing team member.

Flexible, collaborative, proactive, and committed to continuous improvement.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite and related productivity tools.

Skilled in active listening and creating respectful, supportive spaces for individuals to express concerns without judgment or blame.

Physical Requirements
: Ability to travel statewide on a regular basis; lift up to 30 pounds; and bend, stand, or sit for extended periods of time.

Department

FSU Division of University Advancement

Florida State University is currently in the leadership phase of a comprehensive campaign. As the leadership phase of the campaign begins, the Division of University Advancement is accelerating the hiring of additional development officers’ campus-wide and is excited to build out its team. For Advancement professionals who are interested in being a part of a comprehensive campaign from the beginning for their career development, the timing is ideal to join our team.

Responsibilities

The Director of Development will strategically execute a personal solicitation program resulting in philanthropic support by maximizing the identification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of prospects for gifts at the $100,000 and up level for College of Medicine. This individual will secure, on an annual basis, private support at the major and principal gift level and will be responsible for identification of potential major and principal gift prospects. The Director of Development will be expected to uphold and exemplify the operating practices of the Division of University Advancement while raising philanthropic dollars in support of the mission of Florida State University.

Works with the Dean, and the other members of the College of Medicine development, communication and alumni team to develop and execute strategic fundraising initiatives on behalf of the College and the university. Develops, coordinates, and executes an annual major gift plan, implementing fund-raising activities designed to meet an established monetary goal while also hitting a set suite of metrics. Establishes new relationships resulting in philanthropic investment, maintains existing relationships, which may generate new philanthropic investments, and provides prospect/donor-related assessments and feedback for the College and the Division of University Advancement. Identifies, qualifies and sustains a donor prospect portfolio with individuals who have a giving capacity of $500,000 or more. Travels frequently, and secures gifts and pledges from alumni, parents, faculty, staff, and friends of FSU.

Exhibits and promotes a comprehensive understanding of the philanthropic priorities of the College, effectively matching the specific interests of prospects to those needs. Plans for and initiates direct activities with prospects that are designed to enhance the affiliation between the prospect and FSU. Effectively communicates FSU’s mission and represents FSU’s culture to external stakeholders.

Central Development Collaboration

Coordinates with the Division of University Advancement colleagues to identify prospects for planned giving, corporate and foundation giving in partnership with the Office of University Research, and principal giving in partnership with the Principal Gifts Team. Protects confidential information. Perform other related duties as required and assigned.

Administrative/Events

Assists in the facilitation of meetings, communications, informational exchanges and project / philanthropic coordination with the faculty and faculty chairs. new_jobs.description:
Executive Assistant to CEO (Remote)

Are you a dynamic, caring and ridiculously talented executive assistant with experience supporting a Chief Executive Officer (CEO) or President? If so, Boldly is looking for you!

About the position

As an executive assistant and employee of Boldly, you will play the pivotal role of providing top-notch administrative support to senior executives in our client companies. You’ll have the opportunity to choose the clients you support based on company and organizational values and your preferences. These are long-term assignments where you’ll create rewarding partnerships with the executives that you assist while gaining valuable experience in a variety of industries. As a Boldly employee, your work will be 100% remote and flexible with no night or weekend work. This full-time position requires your availability for 40 hours a week during business hours.

As an executive assistant, you’ll apply your professional skills in a wide range of tasks, including:
Maintaining appointment schedules and calendars for senior executives
Planning and scheduling their meetings, conferences, and travel
Making travel arrangements, including flight and hotel bookings
Maximizing the executive’s time by reading, researching, and routing correspondence; drafting letters and documents; collecting and analyzing information; initiating phone calls on their behalf
Managing multiple email inboxes for executives
Managing expenses
Providing customer/supplier support
Other executive admin responsibilities as needed

About you

You have at least 7 years of experience as an Executive Assistant providing high level support to a CEO or President. At this point in your career, your #1 priority is to join a fully remote company that prioritizes flexibility in the workplace, and that promotes a culture of respect, kindness, and transparency. You are a team player with excellent communication skills and a positive outlook who cares deeply about helping others succeed. You take great pride in the quality of your work; you are proactive, dynamic, flexible, tech-savvy, results-oriented, and a stickler for details! And you do it all with a smile!

Required Skills and Experience:

A minimum of 7 years
of direct hands-on experience as an executive assistant with experience supporting a CEO, President, or equivalent C-suite executive. (admin experience as part of another role will not qualify)
Proven ability to manage the complex demands, confidential communications, and fast-paced environment of a CEO or President’s office
Advanced knowledge of online software such as Google Workspace, Office 365, Zoom, and other popular productivity tools
Ability to multitask with ease and prioritize work as needed
Highly organized with excellent time management skills
Ability to learn new tools quickly
Excellent interpersonal communication
Strong writing skills
Ability to work independently and under the pressure of deadlines
Solution driven!

Benefits you’ll love, for full-time employees:

W2 employment status
Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, with 100% of the premium paid by Boldly
401k with employer match
Paid time off including vacation/sick leave
Paid holidays
Paid parental leave
Technology stipend
Ongoing mentoring and support from your Boldly Team Leader to help you succeed with clients

Compensation

The pay rate for this position is $30 per hour with regular raises throughout your tenure. In addition to the hourly pay, total compensation includes benefits such as a 401(k) with employer match.

About our culture

We believe that it is possible to have a rewarding career that doesn’t conflict with your personal responsibilities and life choices. Our diverse team of executive assistants includes parents, military spouses, caregivers, and those with unique life situations where working remotely, part-time or full-time, and on a flexible schedule is not just a ‘nice to have’. Fostering a thriving company culture where everyone has a sense of belonging and enjoys coming to work is our # 1 priority. As a woman-owned business, we are intimately familiar with the challenges women and minorities face in the workplace and we are committed to maintaining a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace for all. And of course, as a remote-first company since our inception in 2012, we are longtime established experts at leading and succeeding in a remote work environment!

As a premium executive assistant company, we set the standard for remote professionals. We’ve been proudly certified as a Great Place to Work for 5 years in a row, and our accolades include being named by Fortune Magazine as one of the “50 Best Small Workplaces”, and Entrepreneur Magazine in their Entrepreneur 360 List as one of the “Best Entrepreneurial Companies in America” and by Color Magazine as a top Inclusive Workplace. Military Friendly named us as part of the Top 10 Military Spouse Friendly Employers, and FlexJobs as a Top 100 Company to watch for. We are also accredited by Flexa as a truly flexible employer.

For more information about our company culture and to hear from team members about their experience in roles filled with variety and new learning - visit our jobs page at https://boldly.com/jobs/

Please note that the requirements in this job description are the minimum qualifications to join our team of premium executive assistants and we are unable to consider any applicant who does not have at least 7 years of experience as an executive assistant. Additionally, Boldly considers LinkedIn to be the foundation of your professional online presence and we require all candidates that wish to apply to have an up-to-date LinkedIn Profile.

At Boldly, we are committed to hiring military spouses and recognize that their unique circumstances pose significant obstacles to employment. We understand that military families often experience frequent relocations and we offer our support by reducing barriers that may hinder their employment. If you are a military spouse with diverse experience, please take care to show us with your detailed resume how your experience aligns with the requirements listed above. By understanding your experience, we can better assess how your background aligns with our open position.

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Founder in Residence

Build a Business. Earn Your First Paying Customer.

Location:
Princeton, New Jersey
Program:
90 Day Founder Residency (In Person)
Work Authorization:
Applicants must already be legally authorized to work in the United States. A Foundery does not provide visa sponsorship.
Relocation:
Founders selected for the residency must be willing to relocate to Princeton, New Jersey for the full 90 Day in-person residency.

Most Careers Reward Your Time.

We Reward What You Build.

A Foundery is a venture studio that builds profitable AI-enabled businesses alongside exceptional founders.
We are not an accelerator.
We are not an incubator.
We are not a co-working space.
We become your venture-building partner.
Our philosophy is simple.
The first paying customer is the first real proof that a business deserves to exist.

Everything we do is designed to help founders reach that milestone faster.

Your Mission

During your first 90 days, you have one objective.
Acquire Your First Paying Customer.

Not raise funding.
Not create a pitch deck.
Not collect followers.
Not build features customers never asked for.
Everything you do should move you closer to your first customer.
Because revenue creates clarity.

What You’ll Do

As the Founder, you own the business.
Your responsibilities include:
Talking to customers.
Understanding real customer problems.
Validating market demand.
Building the right solution.
Winning your first paying customers.
Building lasting customer relationships.
Leading the growth of your company.

No one can build trust with customers better than the founder.
That responsibility cannot be delegated.

What Foundery OS Brings to the Table

While you focus on customers and growing your business,
Foundery OS
provides the operational foundation that allows you to execute faster.

Think of Foundery OS as the operating system behind your company.

Founder Backing
Every successful company begins with someone willing to believe in the founder before the rest of the market does.

At A Foundery, we aim to be those first believers.

We invest in founders with conviction, providing the resources, infrastructure, and operational support needed to transform promising ideas into real businesses.

Our goal is simple:

Help you build a company customers are willing to pay for.

Venture Support

Founder Residency Support during the residency
Early-stage venture funding to validate and build the business
Access to shared business infrastructure
AI platforms and automation tools
Proven operating systems and playbooks
Templates, workflows, and knowledge resources

Business Infrastructure

Professional office space in Princeton
Meeting rooms and collaboration facilities
Business technology and productivity tools

Business Operations

HR support
Recruiting support
Accounting and finance support
Administrative operations
Legal coordination
Back-office support

Founder Development

Weekly founder reviews
Strategic guidance
Accountability and execution coaching
Access to experienced entrepreneurs
Access to advisors and subject matter experts
A community of ambitious founders building alongside you

Our role is simple.
Be your first believers.

Remove operational friction.

Accelerate execution.

Allow founders to spend more time doing what only founders can do: build customer relationships, win business, and grow their companies.

Founder Residency

The Founder Residency is an intensive 90-day venture-building program conducted entirely in person in Princeton, New Jersey.

The residency is conducted under a consulting agreement. Founders who successfully complete the residency and achieve the required milestones may receive a full-time employment offer as Venture Builders.

During the residency, you will participate full-time while building your business.

You will receive:

Up to $11,000 in Founder Residency Support
during the 90-day program to help offset eligible relocation and residency-related expenses while participating full-time in Princeton.
Access to Foundery OS.
Daily collaboration with experienced founders and operators.
AI tools and business systems.
A focused environment designed for execution.

Graduation

Success is measured by outcomes, not attendance.

To successfully complete the Founder Residency, you should demonstrate:
A validated business opportunity.
A solution customers value.
Your first paying customer.
Strong execution.
Founder-level ownership and leadership.

Venture Builder

Founders who successfully complete the residency may receive an offer to join A Foundery as a Venture Builder.

The offer becomes effective upon:
Successful completion of the 90-day residency.
Acquisition of the first paying customer.
Achievement of agreed business milestones.
Mutual agreement to continue building the venture.

Venture Builders may receive:
Annual salary of $80,000 to $120,000

Performance-based bonuses
Long-term leadership opportunities
Continued access to Foundery OS
The opportunity to accumulate significant equity through sustained execution, leadership, and measurable business performance

Our Definition of Success
Our goal isn’t simply to help you complete a 90-day residency.
Our goal is to help you build a company that no longer depends on us.

We work closely together during the first 90 days to help you go from zero to one, from an idea to your first paying customer.

But that is only the beginning.

Over the following months, our objective is to help you build a business with the systems, customers, processes, and leadership needed to operate independently.

Within one year, our goal is for you to be confidently leading your own company, with us becoming an advisor and strategic partner rather than part of your day-to-day operations.
By then, you should have:
Paying customers
Growing recurring revenue
Repeatable operating systems
AI-enabled business processes
A capable team
A sustainable business model
Increasing ownership and long-term wealth creation

Our success is measured by your independence.

When your company can grow because of your leadership, not because of our daily involvement, we know we’ve accomplished our mission.
Our purpose is not to build businesses that depend on A Foundery.
Our purpose is to build founders who can build businesses that thrive without us.

Ownership

Ownership is earned. Not granted.

We believe the people who create long-term value should share meaningfully in the value they create.

As your company grows and you achieve meaningful business milestones, you’ll have the opportunity to accumulate significant equity through sustained execution, leadership, and business performance.

Many founders gradually give up ownership as they raise capital and build their companies.
Our philosophy is different.

Our objective is to help founders increase their ownership as they demonstrate the ability to build, lead, and create lasting enterprise value.

We don’t measure success by how much of your company we own.

We measure success by how much of your company you earn.

When the founder creates more value, the founder should have the opportunity to own more of that value.

That’s why we invest in founders, not just businesses, and build long-term partnerships where incentives remain aligned from the first customer to a successful, independent company.

Who We’re Looking For

We care far more about your ability to build than your résumé.
You may come from software engineering, product management, AI, consulting, operations, finance, sales, entrepreneurship, or another professional background.

What matters most is that you:

Think like an owner.
Learn quickly.
Execute consistently.
Embrace accountability.
Enjoy solving difficult problems.
Are comfortable with uncertainty.
Use AI to increase your effectiveness.
Want to create long-term value rather than simply earn a paycheck.

This Opportunity Is Probably Not For You If

You’re looking for a traditional corporate career.
You prefer predictable routines over building something new.
You avoid accountability.
You’re uncomfortable speaking directly with customers.
You expect guaranteed outcomes without measurable execution.
You aren’t willing to work in person in Princeton during the residency.

Before You Apply

Ask yourself one question.

Would you rather spend the next five years helping build someone else’s company, or spend the next five years building one you could eventually own?

If you’re excited by ownership, accountability, and building something customers are willing to pay for, we’d love to meet you.

Apply today to become a Founder in Residence at A Foundery.
